generic_policy.name: Policy
generic_policy.description: Authoritative statement of organizational intent. Sets the rule. Procedures implement it.
generic_policy.use_when: Establishing organizational rules, requirements, or positions on a topic. The 'what and why'.

standard_sections.label: Purpose
standard_sections.description: Why this policy exists. 2-4 sentences. References the risk or regulatory driver.
standard_sections.example: This policy establishes requirements for controlling access to [Organization]'s information systems to protect against unauthorized access, data breaches, and compliance violations.

standard_sections.label: Scope
standard_sections.description: Who and what this applies to. Systems, people, locations, data types.
standard_sections.example: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, vendors, and third parties with access to [Organization] systems and data.

standard_sections.label: Policy Statement
standard_sections.description: The core rule in plain language. What is required, prohibited, or restricted.
standard_sections.example: It is the policy of [Organization] that all access to information systems shall be granted on a least-privilege basis and reviewed no less than annually.

standard_sections.label: Roles & Responsibilities
standard_sections.description: Who owns, enforces, and complies with this policy. RACI-style.
standard_sections.example: CISO: policy ownership. IT: implementation. HR: workforce sanctions. All Staff: compliance.

standard_sections.label: Procedures
standard_sections.description: High-level how. Detailed steps belong in a SOP, not here.
standard_sections.example: Access requests must be submitted via the ticketing system and approved by the data owner before provisioning.

standard_sections.label: Definitions
standard_sections.description: Key terms used in this policy that need precise meaning.
standard_sections.example: Least Privilege: granting only the minimum access rights necessary to perform assigned job functions.

standard_sections.label: Exceptions
standard_sections.description: How to request an exception. Who approves. How exceptions are tracked.
standard_sections.example: Exceptions to this policy must be submitted to the CISO with business justification and approved in writing.

standard_sections.label: Enforcement
standard_sections.description: Consequences for violations. References sanctions policy.
standard_sections.example: Violations may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination and/or legal action.

standard_sections.label: References
standard_sections.description: Related policies, standards, regulations this policy supports.
standard_sections.example: HIPAA 164.308(a)(3); NIST SP 800-53 AC-2; Access Control Standard

standard_sections.label: Revision History
standard_sections.description: Version, date, author, summary of changes.
standard_sections.example: v1.0 | 01/01/2025 | J. Smith | Initial release

generic_sop.name: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
generic_sop.description: Step-by-step instructions for performing a specific task. Implements a policy. Tells people exactly what to do and when.
generic_sop.use_when: Documenting a repeatable operational process. The 'how, step by step'.

standard_sections.label: Purpose
standard_sections.description: What process this SOP governs. One paragraph.
standard_sections.example: This SOP describes the steps for provisioning and deprovisioning user access to [System].

standard_sections.label: Scope
standard_sections.description: Which systems, teams, and scenarios this SOP covers.
standard_sections.example: Applies to all access provisioning requests for production systems.

standard_sections.label: Roles & Responsibilities
standard_sections.description: Who performs each role in this procedure. Requestor, approver, executor, reviewer.
standard_sections.example: Requestor: submits ticket. Manager: approves. IT Admin: provisions. Security: quarterly review.

standard_sections.label: Prerequisites
standard_sections.description: What must be true before starting this procedure.
standard_sections.example: User must have a signed employment agreement. Manager must be identified in HRIS.

standard_sections.label: Procedure Steps
standard_sections.description: Numbered, sequential steps. Each step: who does what, how, expected outcome.
standard_sections.example:
1. Requestor submits access request in [Ticketing System] with business justification.
2. Manager reviews and approves within 2 business days.
3. IT Admin provisions access within 1 business day of approval.
4. IT Admin closes ticket with confirmation.

standard_sections.label: Exceptions and Escalations
standard_sections.description: What to do when the normal steps don't apply. Who to escalate to.
standard_sections.example: Emergency access: contact on-call security team. Obtain verbal approval, document within 24 hours.

standard_sections.label: Related Documents
standard_sections.description: The policy this SOP implements. Related SOPs.
standard_sections.example: Access Control Policy; Termination Checklist SOP

standard_sections.label: Revision History
standard_sections.description: Version, date, changes.
standard_sections.example: v1.0 | 01/01/2025 | Initial release

generic_playbook.name: Playbook
generic_playbook.description: Event-triggered response guide. Activated when a specific condition occurs — security incident, audit, breach, outage. Tells teams exactly how to respond.
generic_playbook.use_when: Preparing for and responding to specific events. IR playbooks, audit response, breach notification, DR activation.

standard_sections.label: Purpose
standard_sections.description: What event this playbook responds to and what outcome it achieves.
standard_sections.example: This playbook guides the response to a suspected data breach involving PHI.

standard_sections.label: Trigger Conditions
standard_sections.description: What triggers activation of this playbook. Specific, observable conditions.
standard_sections.example: Activate when: unauthorized PHI access is confirmed OR suspected; ransomware is detected; external notification of breach is received.

standard_sections.label: Response Team & RACI
standard_sections.description: Incident commander, responders, communications lead, legal, executive sponsor. Who decides what.
standard_sections.example: Incident Commander: CISO. Technical Lead: Security Engineer. Communications: PR/Legal. Executive Sponsor: CEO.

standard_sections.label: Response Runbook
standard_sections.description: Phase-by-phase steps: Detect → Contain → Investigate → Notify → Recover → Review.
standard_sections.example:
Phase 1 - Contain (0-4hrs): Isolate affected systems. Preserve logs. Notify CISO.
Phase 2 - Investigate (4-24hrs): Determine scope. Identify compromised data.
Phase 3 - Notify (24-72hrs): Legal review. Regulatory notifications if required.

standard_sections.label: Escalation Paths
standard_sections.description: Who to call at each severity level. Contact list with roles.
standard_sections.example: Severity 1 (Critical): Notify CISO immediately. CISO notifies CEO within 1hr.

standard_sections.label: Communication Templates
standard_sections.description: Pre-drafted internal and external communications to adapt.
standard_sections.example: Internal notification template. Customer notification template. Regulator notification template.

standard_sections.label: Supporting Documents
standard_sections.description: IR plan, BCP, contact list, legal counsel info.
standard_sections.example: Incident Response Plan; Business Continuity Plan; Legal Counsel Contact

standard_sections.label: Revision History
standard_sections.description: Version, date, test results, changes.
standard_sections.example: v1.0 | 01/01/2025 | Initial release. Tested 03/2025 — tabletop exercise.

generic_plan.name: Plan
generic_plan.description: Forward-looking document describing how the organization will achieve a security or compliance objective over time. BCP, DR Plan, Risk Management Plan, Security Program Plan.
generic_plan.use_when: Documenting how the org will prepare for, manage, or recover from something. Forward-looking, program-level.

standard_sections.label: Purpose and Objectives
standard_sections.description: What this plan achieves. Measurable objectives.
standard_sections.example: This Business Continuity Plan ensures [Organization] can maintain critical operations during a disruptive event with an RTO of 4 hours and RPO of 1 hour for critical systems.

standard_sections.label: Scope and Assumptions
standard_sections.description: What systems, processes, locations are covered. Key assumptions the plan is built on.
standard_sections.example: Covers all Tier 1 and Tier 2 systems. Assumes primary data center is unavailable. Assumes key personnel are reachable.

standard_sections.label: Roles & Responsibilities
standard_sections.description: Who owns the plan, who activates it, who executes it.
standard_sections.example: Plan Owner: CISO. Activation Authority: CEO or CISO. Recovery Teams: IT, Operations, HR.

standard_sections.label: Risk and Impact Assessment
standard_sections.description: Threats this plan addresses. Business impact if not addressed.
standard_sections.example: Key threats: ransomware, natural disaster, key personnel loss. Estimated impact per hour of downtime: $X.

standard_sections.label: Recovery / Response Procedures
standard_sections.description: Step-by-step procedures for activation, execution, and recovery. Organized by scenario or phase.
standard_sections.example: Activation: Incident declared by CEO or CISO. Recovery Team lead notified within 15 min. War room established.

standard_sections.label: Testing and Maintenance Schedule
standard_sections.description: How often the plan is tested, by whom, and how results are documented and actioned.
standard_sections.example: Annual tabletop exercise (Q1). Annual failover test (Q3). Plan reviewed and updated annually or after activation.

standard_sections.label: Supporting Documents
standard_sections.description: System inventory, contact lists, vendor contracts, related plans.
standard_sections.example: System Recovery Runbooks; Vendor Contact List; Incident Response Plan

standard_sections.label: Revision History
standard_sections.description: Version, date, test outcomes, changes.
standard_sections.example: v1.0 | 01/01/2025 | Initial release. v1.1 | 04/2025 | Updated after tabletop.

generic_standard.name: Standard
generic_standard.description: Technical baseline. Defines specific, measurable requirements that implement a policy. Password standard, encryption standard, logging standard.
generic_standard.use_when: Setting specific technical requirements underneath a policy. More prescriptive than a policy, less procedural than a SOP.

standard_sections.label: Purpose
standard_sections.description: What this standard governs and which policy it implements.
standard_sections.example: This standard defines minimum password requirements for all [Organization] systems, implementing the Access Control Policy.

standard_sections.label: Applicability
standard_sections.description: Which systems, accounts, and users this standard applies to. Any exclusions.
standard_sections.example: Applies to all user accounts on [Organization] systems. Service accounts governed by Privileged Access Standard.

standard_sections.label: Requirements
standard_sections.description: Numbered, specific, measurable technical requirements. Each one testable.
standard_sections.example:
1. Passwords must be minimum 14 characters.
2. Passwords must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.
3. Password reuse prohibited for last 12 passwords.
4. MFA required for all remote access.

standard_sections.label: Exception Process
standard_sections.description: How to get an exception to a specific requirement. Compensating controls.
standard_sections.example: Legacy systems unable to meet MFA requirement must document compensating controls and receive CISO approval annually.

standard_sections.label: Compliance Measurement
standard_sections.description: How compliance with this standard is measured and reported.
standard_sections.example: Compliance assessed quarterly via automated scan. Non-compliant systems reported to system owner within 5 days.

standard_sections.label: References
standard_sections.description: Policy this implements. Related standards. Regulatory citations.
standard_sections.example: Access Control Policy; NIST SP 800-63B; PCI DSS Requirement 8.3

standard_sections.label: Revision History
standard_sections.description: Version, date, changes.
standard_sections.example: v1.0 | 01/01/2025 | Initial release
